evals.prompt: Write a Qwilr proposal for a $50k annual SaaS deal to a VP of Marketing at a mid-market ecommerce company. They saw a demo last week and want a formal proposal with interactive pricing.
evals.expected_output: A complete proposal page blueprint with section-by-section copy including executive summary, problem/solution framing, scope of work, an interactive quote block configuration with line items, and a clear CTA. Should include Qwilr-specific features like optional add-ons in the quote block.

assertions.name: has_page_blueprint
assertions.description: Includes a section-by-section structure mapped to Qwilr block types

assertions.name: has_actual_copy
assertions.description: Writes real proposal copy for each section, not just placeholders

assertions.name: has_quote_block_config
assertions.description: Designs an interactive quote block with line items, pricing, and optional add-ons

assertions.name: has_buyer_context
assertions.description: Copy references the buyer's role (VP Marketing), industry (ecommerce), and deal stage (post-demo)

evals.prompt: I need to create a Qwilr pricing table for a consulting engagement with Brightline Retail, a mid-market ecommerce company. Three tiers: (1) Basic Audit at $15k — review their current marketing stack and deliver a recommendations report, (2) Full Implementation at $45k — migrate them to our platform, set up integrations, and train their team over 8 weeks, (3) Ongoing Retainer at $8k/month — managed services, monthly reporting, and optimization. Optional add-ons: custom analytics dashboard ($5k) and API integration with their ERP ($12k). The client should be able to pick which tiers and add-ons they want.
evals.expected_output: A quote block configuration with three sections or optional line items representing the three tiers, each with descriptions, fixed costs, and isOptional settings. Should include guidance on how to structure tiers so the buyer can self-select.

assertions.name: has_three_tiers
assertions.description: Includes three distinct pricing tiers (audit, implementation, retainer)

assertions.name: has_optional_items
assertions.description: Uses isOptional or similar mechanism to let the buyer select which tiers they want

assertions.name: has_pricing_details
assertions.description: Each tier has a name, description, and price configuration

evals.prompt: Build a Qwilr proposal page for a renewal deal. We sell a customer success platform (CSaaS) and we've been working with NovaTech Solutions, a 500-person B2B SaaS company, for a year. They're renewing their $80k annual contract — in year one we helped them reduce churn by 18% and increase NPS from 32 to 51. We want to upsell an additional Analytics Pro module worth $30k/year that gives them predictive churn scoring and revenue forecasting. Their VP of Customer Success is our main contact and the CFO needs to sign off on the expansion.
evals.expected_output: A renewal-focused proposal that leads with results achieved in year one, then presents the expansion scope and pricing. Structure should differ from a new-logo proposal — lighter on problem framing, heavier on proven value and expansion opportunity.

assertions.name: renewal_structure
assertions.description: Leads with results/value delivered, not problem/solution framing

assertions.name: has_expansion_pricing
assertions.description: Includes both renewal base and add-on expansion items in pricing

assertions.name: uses_qwilr_features
assertions.description: Leverages Qwilr-specific features like optional add-ons for the expansion items
